---
title: "Turkey Tangle Frogfruit (Phyla nodiflora)"
slug: "phyla-nodiflora"
group: "Plantae"
image: "https://inaturalist-open-data.s3.amazonaws.com/photos/180774974/medium.jpg"        # keep for thumbnails
image-alt: "Turkey Tangle Frogfruit"  # keep for a11y
tax_kingdom: "Plantae"
tax_class: "Magnoliopsida"
tax_order: "Lamiales"
tax_family: "Verbenaceae"
categories: ["Plantae", "Magnoliopsida", "Lamiales", "Verbenaceae"]   # these drive the chips + filtering
freeze: true
---

<figure class="species-figure">

<img src="https://inaturalist-open-data.s3.amazonaws.com/photos/622183583/medium.jpg" alt="Turkey Tangle Frogfruit" class="species-photo"/>

<figcaption class="photo-credit">

Photo: <a href="https://www.inaturalist.org/people/betseyboughton">
betseyboughton</a> (CC BY-NC) via <a href="https://inaturalist-open-data.s3.amazonaws.com/photos/180774974/medium.jpg0">iNaturalist</a>

</figcaption>

</figure>

## Taxonomy

-   **Class:** Magnoliopsida
-   **Order:** Lamiales
-   **Family:** Verbenaceae

## Occurrence at DeLuca Preserve

-   [DeLuca Bio Blitz Species Observations](https://www.inaturalist.org/observations?project_id=191727&subview=map&taxon_id=59040)

## Conservation & rarity context

-   IUCN: Not Listed
-   **Rarity note:** Common species in Florida found in a variety of habitats including lawns, roadsides, coastal areas, and disturbed sites.

## Natural history notes

A low-growing, mat-forming plant characterized by its creeping stems, opposite leaves, and small, button-like flower heads with white to pale purple petals. In Florida, it is commonly found in a variety of habitats including lawns, roadsides, coastal areas, and disturbed sites. It grows during the warmer months and spreads readily, often forming dense groundcover that provides nectar for pollinators.

## References / links

-   [iNaturalist species page](https://www.inaturalist.org/taxa/59040-Phyla-nodiflora)
-   [Local profile](https://www.flawildflowers.org/flower-friday-phyla-nodiflora/)
